text:
The paper is clearly real and well-documented across multiple authoritative sources. The Springer page, DBLP, ACM DL, and the paper's own PDF all confirm authorship as **Nicholas Mattei and Toby Walsh**. The automated checker's metadata mismatch (listing only "T. Walsh") is a Semantic Scholar indexing artifact — the checker matched an incomplete database entry. Every other authoritative source (Springer, DBLP, ACM DL, the actual PDF) confirms both authors.

EXPLANATION: The paper "PrefLib: A Library for Preferences" by Nicholas Mattei and Toby Walsh (2013) is confirmed on Springer, DBLP, ACM DL, and the authors' own PDF; the automated checker's single-author record is a Semantic Scholar metadata deficiency, not an error in the cited reference.
LINK: https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-642-41575-3_20
FOUND_TITLE: PrefLib: A Library for Preferences http://www.preflib.org
FOUND_AUTHORS: Nicholas Mattei, Toby Walsh
FOUND_YEAR: 2013
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
